title: Judge of 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
subhead: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that held the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.

title: Spain picks apart Mbappé's powerless France in World Cup semi-final
Wildfires that ravaged historic Fontainebleau forest outside Paris 'contained'
Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
subhead: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.

title: Ukraine expands attacks in Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
subhead: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

title: If you love Eugène Boudin, you will love Honfleur
subhead: The Impressionist painter has a museum named after him in this popular Normandy tourist town, where he was born. Many other artists have been drawn here by the ever-changing light of the estuary.

title: What does France's assisted dying bill contain, as MPs prepare to cast their final vote?
subhead: As it reaches the end of its parliamentary journey with Wednesday's vote in the  Assemblée Nationale, the proposed law would establish the right to assisted suicide or euthanasia for patients experiencing constant suffering from serious and incurable illnesses, under strictly regulated conditions.

title: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
subhead: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
